page: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor running at 2.3 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. Because hyperthreading is disabled, the instance maintains a strict 1.0 thread per core ratio. The hardware profile is completed by 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is rated at a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth, accompanied by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also strong, supported by a high PassMark memory score. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Cryptographic and compression workloads show mixed results, with single-threaded bzip3 decompression performing strongly, while gzip compression and PassMark encryption tests register average speeds.

With dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers predictable performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core options. This makes it a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring stable CPU allocation and fast local storage without the premium of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose web serving, text processing, software compilation, database operations, and light language model inference using small to medium models.
description: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 AMD EPYC processors. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating at 2.3 GHz without hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance and memory write speeds, both ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core execution and database operations fall within the average range. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable performance without resource contention. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and small-scale database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB of RAM, a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with strong single-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server with balanced memory, local NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with no hyperthreading
32 GB of system memory and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and uncached memory write performance
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
